'''James Cunningham''' (born 30 April 1994) is an English former [[rugby league]] footballer who last played as a {{rlp|hk}} for [[York Knights]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].

He has played for [[Hull F.C.]] in the [[Super League]], and on loan from [[Hull F.C.|Hull]] at the [[London Broncos]] in the top flight and [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]] in the [[2015 RFL Championship|Championship]]. He also played for [[London Broncos|London]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]] and the Super League before joining the [[Toronto Wolfpack]] in the [[Super League|top flight]]. At the [[London Broncos|Broncos]] he occasionally played as a {{rlp|sh}} and {{rlp|so}}. He joined Toulouse from the [[Huddersfield Giants]].

==Background==
Cunningham was born in [[Kingston upon Hull]], East Riding of Yorkshire, England.

==Career==
===Hull F.C.===
He played for [[Hull F.C.]] in the [[Super League]], and on loan from [[Hull F.C.|Hull]] at the [[London Broncos]] in the top flight and [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]] in the [[2015 RFL Championship|Championship]].{{citation needed|date=January 2023}}

===London Broncos===
The [[London Broncos]] paid an undisclosed fee to [[Hull F.C.]] in 2015. It was his second stint at the [[London Broncos]] after being on loan there in the 2014 season in Super League.
[[File:James Cunningham London Broncos.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Cunningham playing for the London Broncos in 2016]]

On 26 October 2018, Cunningham signed a one-year deal with the [[London Broncos]] keeping him at the club until the end of the 2019 season.{{citation needed|date=January 2023}}

===Toronto Wolfpack===
Cunningham left London to sign for the [[Toronto Wolfpack]] during 2019.{{citation needed|date=January 2023}}

===Huddersfield Giants===
On 9 November 2020, it was announced that Cunningham would join [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] from the 2021 season on a two-year deal.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/54871945|title=James Cunningham: Huddersfield Giants sign Toronto Wolfpack hooker|work=BBC Sport|accessdate=18 Dec 2020}}</ref>

===Toulouse Olympique===
On 16 Oct 2021 it was reported that he had signed for [[Toulouse Olympique]] in the [[Super League]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=16 Oct 2021|accessdate=16 Oct 2021|title=Toulouse snap up James Cunningham|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/toulouse-snap-up-james-cunningham/}}</ref> He was signed on a two-year contract until the end of the 2023 season.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.to13.com/actualites/james-cunningham-signe-au-to/|title = James CUNNINGHAM signe au TO|date = 19 October 2021}}</ref>

===York Knights===
In February 2023, it was reported that Cunningham had joined [[York Knights]] on a two-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/james-cunningham-signs-two-year-deal-with-championship-club |title=James Cunningham signs two-year deal with Championship club |website=Love Rugby League |date=1 February 2023 |access-date=27 May 2026}}</ref> He announced his retirement at the end of 2024.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/former-hull-fc-man-announces-retirement-at-the-age-of-just-30 |title=Former Hull FC man announces retirement at the age of just 30 |website=Love Rugby League |date=27 December 2024 |access-date=27 May 2026}}</ref>

==International career==
In July 2018 he was selected in the [[England Knights]] Performance squad.<ref name="Ten new additions in England Knights squad">{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/ten-new-additions-in-england-knights-squad/|title=Ten new additions in England Knights squad|publisher=loverugbyleague|date=24 July 2018|accessdate=31 October 2019}}</ref>
